issues_found: Source is a landing page/table of contents (listing_page), which violates Rule 5 - homepage, navigation_page, listing_page, and search_page types must be rejected
event_type is set to 'unknown' when 'monetary_policy_report' would be the correct classification given the Bank of Canada Annual Report covering inflation and monetary policy
Extracted content is extremely thin - only table of contents structure and minimal descriptive headings
No substantive policy analysis, data tables, or detailed discussion present in this extraction
Published date remains 'unknown' despite this being a historical 2022 document
Record has no actionable research value as-is; full PDF content needed for proper archival
human_review_reason: Rule 5 is clear: listing_page sources must be rejected. This record should not proceed to human review.

corrected_fields.summary: This is the Bank of Canada's Annual Report 2022 landing page, containing only a table of contents and minimal descriptive text. The extracted content confirms that Canadian inflation rose from just under 5% to 8% in June 2022 before falling to just over 6% by year-end, and that the Bank undertook its most rapid tightening cycle in Canada's history. The substantive content of the annual report is not present in this extraction.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This source is too thin to provide actionable research value. It is a navigation page for the Bank of Canada Annual Report 2022, not the report itself. The only substantive information is high-level inflation trajectory and the characterization of the 2022 tightening cycle as the most rapid in Canadian history.
corrected_fields.macro_context: Historical record of Canadian inflation trajectory in 2022 and Bank of Canada monetary policy response. The data confirms inflation peaked at 8% in June 2022 and retreated to just over 6% by year-end, with the Bank responding through aggressive rate hikes.
